commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a962b9362d25d138d1ea5c4a199f1154900c1ee2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR defers local-model lean filtering while Code Mode or Tool Search catalogs are built, reapplies final visible filtering afterward, adds focused tests, and updates local-model docs.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +83, Docs 0. Total +109 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main filters `localModelLean` tools before catalog compaction, so lean-denied tools cannot enter the catalog. I did not run tests in this read-only review, but the branch adds focused tests for pre-catalog and final visible filtering.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR is protected/maintainer-owned and needs human acceptance of the compatibility contract plus a small config-help correction before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): keep lean tools behind catalog controls
target.body:
Summary:
- Keep `localModelLean` from removing heavyweight tools before Code Mode or Tool Search can place them behind compact catalog controls.
- Defer the construction-time lean filter only for catalog-enabled runs, then keep the final visible lean filter so `browser`, `cron`, and `message` stay out of the model-visible schema.
- Add focused coverage for pre-catalog vs final visible filtering, tool-construction deferral, and update local-model docs.

Verification:
- `git diff --check $(git merge-base HEAD origin/main)..HEAD`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/experimental-features.md docs/gateway/local-models.md docs/providers/ollama.md`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-tools.ts src/agents/agent-tools-agent-config.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/local-model-lean-catalog.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/agent-tools.ts src/agents/agent-tools-agent-config.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/local-model-lean-catalog.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean-catalog.test.ts` - 1 file / 3 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean-catalog.test.ts` - 2 files / 9 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean-catalog.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools-agent-config.test.ts` - 4 files / 57 tests passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean after fixing the first review finding.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_a2d372f1ed4d`, slug `crimson-shrimp`, run `run_2d50fd073375`, `pnpm check:changed` passed, exit `0`, cleanup `leaseStopped=true`.

Behavior addressed: lean local-model runs no longer lose policy-allowed `browser`, `cron`, and `message` before compact catalog controls can represent those tools cheaply.
Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux runner, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_a2d372f1ed4d`, run `run_2d50fd073375`, branch head `b4f0b003223a1fcd8e5364a17a8e1d493b06eb85`, changed gate from base `91ecd9645f12c10234b50f1e09ac82ee6e8cae6c`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: checked the rebased diff, checked docs formatting for the touched docs, checked TypeScript format/lint for touched source, ran focused local Vitest files, ran autoreview, and ran remote `pnpm check:changed` through AWS Crabbox.
Evidence after fix: local targeted tests passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; Crabbox changed gate passed with exit `0`.
Observed result after fix: lean mode keeps heavyweight tools available to compact catalog controls before the final model-visible filtering step, while the final visible schema still excludes heavyweight tools for local lean models.
What was not tested: Windows, WSL2, and a real Ollama daemon. GitHub CI currently has an unrelated latest-main failure in `check-additional-boundaries-bcd` from `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/api/token.ts:224`; this branch does not touch `extensions/qqbot`, and latest `origin/main` still contains the same raw `fetch()` guard violation.

Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599
target.author: vincentkoc
